title: if/else if chains and switch cases should avoid having the same condition
description:
Duplicate conditions in if/else if chains and switch statements can lead to unreachable code, logic errors, and maintenance issues. When the same condition appears multiple times in a conditional construct, only the first occurrence will be evaluated, making subsequent identical conditions unreachable.

This creates several problems:
- Dead code that never executes
- Confusion about intended logic
- Maintenance difficulties when updating conditions
- Potential logic bugs where different actions are intended for the same condition

This rule detects:
- Duplicate conditions in if/else if chains
- Duplicate case values in switch statements
- Complex expressions that evaluate to the same logical condition
- Both exact matches and semantically equivalent conditions
recommendation:
Remove or consolidate duplicate conditions:
- Merge duplicate if/else if conditions into a single condition with combined logic
- Remove duplicate switch cases and combine their logic
- Use logical operators (&&, ||) to combine related conditions
- Consider refactoring complex conditional logic into separate functions
- Ensure each condition serves a distinct purpose
impact: Unreachable code, logic errors, and increased maintenance complexity

noncompliant.code: if (status === 'active') { return 'Active'; } else if (status === 'inactive') { return 'Inactive'; } else if (status === 'active') { return 'Really active'; }
noncompliant.description: Duplicate condition in if/else if chain - third condition never executes

noncompliant.code: switch (code) { case 404: return 'Not Found'; case 500: return 'Server Error'; case 404: return 'Page Missing'; }
noncompliant.description: Duplicate case in switch statement - second case 404 never executes

noncompliant.code: if (user.role === 'admin' && action === 'delete') { return true; } else if (user.role === 'moderator') { return false; } else if (user.role === 'admin' && action === 'delete') { return false; }
noncompliant.description: Duplicate complex condition with different intended behavior

compliant.code: if (status === 'active') { return handleActive(); } else if (status === 'inactive') { return handleInactive(); } else { return handleUnknown(); }
compliant.description: No duplicate conditions, each branch is reachable

compliant.code: switch (code) { case 404: case 410: return 'Not Found'; case 500: return 'Server Error'; default: return 'Unknown'; }
compliant.description: Using fall-through cases instead of duplicates for similar handling

compliant.code: if (user.role === 'admin' && (action === 'delete' || action === 'modify')) { return true; } else { return false; }
compliant.description: Combined conditions using logical operators instead of duplicates

checks.message: Duplicate condition detected. Remove or consolidate duplicate conditions in if/else chains and switch cases.
